name: Michele Fitzgerald
signature_move: Won the final reward challenge to earn the unprecedented power to remove a juror, strategically eliminating Neal Gottlieb from the jury to neutralize his potential advocacy for Aubry at Final Tribal Council.
strategy_summary: Michele played one of the most socially dominant winning games in Survivor history, building genuine personal connections that translated into jury votes despite being perceived as a lesser strategic threat than finalist Aubry Bracco. She avoided becoming a target through most of the post-merge by staying adaptable rather than dictating votes, pivoting from the women's alliance to side voting positions as needed. Her late-game immunity run and the jury removal advantage were clutch moves that secured her path to a decisive 5-2-0 victory.
fun_fact: At 24, Michele was the youngest winner since Jud 'Fabio' Birza, and her win sparked one of the most polarizing Survivor debates ever — fans and even host Jeff Probst openly questioned whether the 'right person' won over strategic powerhouse Aubry Bracco, making Michele the poster child for the social game's value in Survivor.
